message:
chore(beads): close bd-aosn — recovery docs added to homebrew-tap and scoop-bucket

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-abj4","title":"Create ntm session spawning wrapper","description":"# Task: ntm session spawning wrapper

## What
Bash function to spawn an ntm session for a given repo with a given prompt.

## How
- `ntm new -n "ai-sync-<repo-name>" -d "<repo-path>"`
- Send prompt via ntm's stdin mechanism or tmux send-keys
- Wait for completion or timeout

## Interface
`spawn_ai_session <repo_path> <prompt_file> [timeout_seconds]`

## Considerations
- Session naming: avoid collisions, allow cleanup
- Timeout handling for stuck sessions
- Output capture for logging","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-14T07:03:38.412908142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-14T08:01:51.054071721Z","closed_at":"2026-01-14T08:01:51.054071721Z","close_reason":"Implemented spawn_ai_session, is_session_active, and kill_ai_session functions for ntm integration","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0}
 {"id":"bd-acpv","title":"prune --delete: avoid prompting without TTY","description":"Fresh-eyes audit: cmd_prune --delete currently prompts even when stdin isn't a TTY, which can hang in pipelines/automation. Make it require a TTY for confirmation (unless --non-interactive), and ensure prompts go to stderr using printf/IFS= read.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-05T17:32:22.399674246Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T17:33:05.604914002Z","closed_at":"2026-01-05T17:33:05.604914002Z","close_reason":"Avoid prompting for prune deletion without a TTY; gum confirm only when stdout is TTY; prompts use printf/IFS= read","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0}
 {"id":"bd-ai1z","title":"Phase 2: Worktree Preparation & Isolation","description":"# Phase 2: Worktree Preparation & Isolation

## Objective
Create isolated git worktrees for each repository being reviewed and prepare repo digest caches for incremental understanding. This prevents AI edits from affecting the main working tree and enables safe parallel reviews.

## Implementation Requirements

### 2.1 Worktree Creation
```bash
prepare_review_worktrees() {
    local repos=("$@")
    local base="$RU_STATE_DIR/worktrees/$REVIEW_RUN_ID"
    mkdir -p "$base"

    for repo_info in "${repos[@]}"; do
        # Parse repo spec
        local url branch custom_name local_path repo_id
        resolve_repo_spec "$repo_spec" ...

        # Refuse to run on dirty trees
        ensure_clean_or_fail "$local_path"

        local wt_path="$base/${repo_id//\//_}"
        local wt_branch="ru/review/$REVIEW_RUN_ID/${repo_id//\//-}"

        # Fetch latest and create worktree
        git -C "$local_path" fetch --quiet
        git -C "$local_path" worktree add -b "$wt_branch" "$wt_path" "$base_ref"

        # Create .ru directory for artifacts
        mkdir -p "$wt_path/.ru"

        record_worktree_mapping "$repo_id" "$wt_path" "$wt_branch"
    done
}
```

### 2.2 Worktree Mapping File
Store mapping in `$RU_STATE_DIR/worktrees/$RUN_ID/mapping.json`:
```json
{
  "owner/repo": {
    "worktree_path": "/path/to/worktree",
    "branch": "ru/review/20250108-1200/owner-repo",
    "base_ref": "main",
    "created_at": "2025-01-08T12:00:00Z"
  }
}
```

### 2.3 Portable Locking
Use flock for concurrent mapping updates:
```bash
update_mapping_with_lock() {
    (
        flock -x 200
        # Update mapping.json atomically
    ) 200>"$mapping_lockfile"
}
```

### 2.4 Branch Pin Support
Respect branch pins from repo spec (e.g., `owner/repo@develop`).
The worktree should check out the pinned ref as base.

### 2.5 Dirty Tree Handling
```bash
ensure_clean_or_fail() {
    local repo_path="$1"
    if ! git -C "$repo_path" diff --quiet HEAD; then
        log_error "$repo_path has uncommitted changes"
        return 1
    fi
}
```

---

## REPO DIGEST CACHE (Critical P1 Feature)

The repo digest cache eliminates repetitive "understand the codebase" work by persisting Claude's understanding across runs.

### Cache Structure
```
~/.local/state/ru/repo-digests/
├── owner_repo.md           # Cached digest content
├── owner_repo.meta.json    # Cache metadata
├── another_repo.md
└── another_repo.meta.json
```

### Cache Metadata Schema
```json
{
  "repo": "owner/repo",
  "last_commit": "abc123def456",
  "last_review_at": "2025-01-08T12:00:00Z",
  "digest_version": 1,
  "context_lines": 450,
  "key_files": ["src/main.py", "lib/core.py"],
  "patterns_detected": ["async", "factory", "repository"]
}
```

### Digest Preparation Flow
```bash
prepare_repo_digests() {
    local repos=("$@")
    local cache_dir="$RU_STATE_DIR/repo-digests"
    mkdir -p "$cache_dir"

    for repo_info in "${repos[@]}"; do
        local repo_id wt_path
        get_worktree_mapping "$repo_info" repo_id wt_path

        local digest_cache="$cache_dir/${repo_id//\//_}.md"
        local meta_cache="$cache_dir/${repo_id//\//_}.meta.json"

        if [[ -f "$digest_cache" ]] && [[ -f "$meta_cache" ]]; then
            # Copy cached digest to worktree
            cp "$digest_cache" "$wt_path/.ru/repo-digest.md"

            # Compute delta since last review
            local last_sha
            last_sha=$(jq -r '.last_commit' "$meta_cache")
            local current_sha
            current_sha=$(git -C "$wt_path" rev-parse HEAD)

            if [[ "$last_sha" != "$current_sha" ]]; then
                # Append changes section
                local changes
                changes=$(git -C "$wt_path" log --oneline "$last_sha..$current_sha" 2>/dev/null | head -20)
                if [[ -n "$changes" ]]; then
                    echo -e "\n## Changes Since Last Review\n\`\`\`\n$changes\n\`\`\`" >> "$wt_path/.ru/repo-digest.md"
                fi
                log_info "Loaded cached digest for $repo_id (with delta)"
            else
                log_info "Loaded cached digest for $repo_id (no changes)"
            fi
        else
            log_info "No cached digest for $repo_id - agent will create fresh"
        fi
    done
}
```

### Digest Cache Update (Post-Review)
```bash
update_digest_cache() {
    local repo_id="$1"
    local wt_path="$2"

    local cache_dir="$RU_STATE_DIR/repo-digests"
    mkdir -p "$cache_dir"

    local digest_file="$wt_path/.ru/repo-digest.md"
    if [[ -f "$digest_file" ]]; then
        local current_sha
        current_sha=$(git -C "$wt_path" rev-parse HEAD)

        # Copy digest to cache
        cp "$digest_file" "$cache_dir/${repo_id//\//_}.md"

        # Write metadata
        jq -n \
            --arg repo "$repo_id" \
            --arg sha "$current_sha" \
            --arg ts "$(date -Iseconds)" \
            '{
                repo: $repo,
                last_commit: $sha,
                last_review_at: $ts,
                digest_version: 1
            }' > "$cache_dir/${repo_id//\//_}.meta.json"

        log_info "Updated digest cache for $repo_id"
    fi
}
```

---

## Unit Tests (scripts/test_unit_worktree.sh)

1. **test_worktree_creation**: Verify worktree created at correct path
2. **test_worktree_branch_naming**: Verify branch follows convention
3. **test_worktree_mapping_recorded**: Verify mapping.json updated
4. **test_worktree_dirty_reject**: Verify dirty repo rejection
5. **test_worktree_branch_pin**: Verify branch pins respected
6. **test_mapping_concurrent_updates**: Verify lock prevents corruption
7. **test_ru_directory_created**: Verify .ru/ dir exists in worktree
8. **test_worktree_fetch_before_create**: Verify fetch runs first
9. **test_digest_cache_copy**: Verify cached digest copied to worktree
10. **test_digest_delta_computation**: Verify changes section appended
11. **test_digest_cache_update**: Verify cache updated after review
12. **test_digest_metadata_schema**: Verify metadata JSON valid

## E2E Tests (scripts/test_e2e_worktree.sh)

1. **test_full_worktree_lifecycle**:
   - Create worktree
   - Make commits
   - Cleanup worktree
   - Verify branch removed

2. **test_parallel_worktree_creation**:
   - Create 5 worktrees in parallel
   - Verify all mapping entries valid
   - Verify no race conditions

3. **test_worktree_recovery_after_interrupt**:
   - Simulate interrupt during creation
   - Resume should detect partial state
   - Complete creation correctly

4. **test_digest_cache_round_trip**:
   - First run: no cache, agent creates digest
   - Cache stored after completion
   - Second run: cache loaded with delta
   - Verify incremental understanding works

## Logging Requirements
- LOG_DEBUG: "Creating worktree for $repo_id at $wt_path"
- LOG_DEBUG: "Using base ref: $base_ref (pinned: $is_pinned)"
- LOG_INFO: "Created worktree for $repo_id"
- LOG_WARN: "$repo_id has dirty working tree"
- LOG_ERROR: "Failed to create worktree: $error"
- LOG_INFO: "Loaded cached digest for $repo_id (with delta)"
- LOG_INFO: "No cached digest for $repo_id - agent will create fresh"
- LOG_INFO: "Updated digest cache for $repo_id"
- LOG_DEBUG: "Digest delta: $num_commits commits since last review"

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Worktrees created in isolated directory
- [ ] Branch naming follows convention
- [ ] Mapping file updated atomically
- [ ] Dirty trees rejected with clear message
- [ ] Branch pins respected
- [ ] Concurrent creation is safe
- [ ] .ru directory created for artifacts
- [ ] Digest cache loaded when available
- [ ] Delta computed and appended to cached digest
- [ ] Cache updated after successful review
- [ ] All 12 unit tests pass
- [ ] All 4 e2e tests pass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-08T06:24:11.518884097Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T16:21:45.173631256Z","closed_at":"2026-01-08T16:21:45.173631256Z","close_reason":"Phase 2 Worktree Preparation complete: all unit and e2e tests passing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ai1z","depends_on_id":"bd-egh3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-08T06:28:52.380537607Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-aosn","title":"Document Rollback and Recovery Procedures for Package Distribution","description":"# Document Rollback and Recovery Procedures for Package Distribution

## Background

The auto-update system can potentially introduce problems:
1. Bad checksum fetched (corrupted release, wrong file)
2. Invalid formula/manifest committed (syntax error)
3. Release withdrawn after manifest updated
4. Hash mismatch errors for users
5. Binary incompatibility discovered after release

Currently, there is NO documented procedure for:
- Rolling back a bad formula/manifest
- Communicating issues to users
- Preventing cascading failures
- Manual recovery steps

## Goals

Create comprehensive documentation and tooling for:
1. Detecting problems (monitoring)
2. Rolling back bad updates
3. Preventing bad updates from being deployed
4. Communicating with users about issues
5. Post-incident analysis

## Recovery Procedures

### Procedure 1: Rolling Back a Bad Formula (Homebrew)

```bash
# 1. Identify the bad commit
cd /data/projects/homebrew-tap
git log --oneline Formula/<tool>.rb

# 2. Revert to previous version
git revert HEAD  # If latest commit is bad
# OR
git revert <commit-sha>  # Specific bad commit

# 3. Verify formula is valid
brew audit --strict --online Formula/<tool>.rb

# 4. Push the revert
git push origin main

# 5. Verify users can install
brew update
brew install dicklesworthstone/tap/<tool>
```

### Procedure 2: Rolling Back a Bad Manifest (Scoop)

```bash
# 1. Identify the bad commit
cd /data/projects/scoop-bucket
git log --oneline <tool>.json

# 2. Revert to previous version
git revert HEAD

# 3. Validate JSON syntax
jq . <tool>.json > /dev/null

# 4. Push the revert
git push origin main

# 5. Verify users can install
scoop update
scoop install dicklesworthstone/<tool>
```

### Procedure 3: Pinning to Known-Good Version

If a source release is bad, pin to previous version:

```bash
# Homebrew: Edit formula to use older release
# In Formula/<tool>.rb, change:
version "0.1.55"  # Change from 0.1.56 to 0.1.55
# And revert URLs and hashes to match

# Add comment explaining why pinned:
# PINNED: v0.1.56 has critical bug, see https://github.com/.../issues/123
```

### Procedure 4: Emergency Disable Tool

If a tool cannot be fixed quickly, disable installation:

```ruby
# Homebrew: Add disable directive
class Tool < Formula
  disable! because: "Critical vulnerability discovered, see https://..."
  # ... rest of formula
end
```

```json
// Scoop: Rename or add deprecated field
{
    "version": "0.1.55",
    "##_deprecated": "Critical vulnerability discovered - DO NOT INSTALL",
    // ...
}
```

## Prevention Mechanisms

### 1. Pre-Commit Validation Hook

Add to both repos:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Validate all changed formulas
for formula in $(git diff --cached --name-only | grep 'Formula/.*\.rb$'); do
    ruby -c "$formula" || exit 1
    # Additional validation...
done

# Validate all changed manifests
for manifest in $(git diff --cached --name-only | grep '\.json$'); do
    jq . "$manifest" > /dev/null || exit 1
done
```

### 2. CI Validation Before Merge

Workflow that:
1. Validates syntax
2. Checks URLs are reachable
3. Verifies checksums match
4. Runs test installation in container

### 3. Staged Rollout (Future Enhancement)

Consider adding "beta" channel:
- New updates go to beta first
- After 24h with no issues, promote to stable
- Users can opt-in to beta: `brew install --HEAD`

## Monitoring & Alerting

### 1. Installation Failure Detection

Parse Homebrew analytics (if available) or:
- Monitor GitHub issues for "install failed"
- Set up Sentry/error tracking in update scripts

### 2. Health Check Endpoint

Consider adding a health check that verifies:
- All formulas have valid syntax
- All manifests have valid JSON
- All referenced URLs are reachable

```yaml
# .github/workflows/health-check.yml
name: Health Check
on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 */6 * * *'  # Every 6 hours
jobs:
  check:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Validate all formulas
        run: |
          for f in Formula/*.rb; do
            ruby -c "$f" || echo "BROKEN: $f"
          done
      - name: Check URLs reachable
        run: |
          # Extract URLs and check with curl
```

## Communication Templates

### GitHub Issue Template for Known Problems

```markdown
## ⚠️ Known Issue: [Tool] v[Version]

**Status**: Under Investigation / Fix in Progress / Resolved

**Symptoms**:
- Installation fails with error: ...
- Hash mismatch when installing

**Workaround**:
\`\`\`bash
# Install previous version
brew install dicklesworthstone/tap/<tool>@0.1.54
\`\`\`

**Timeline**:
- [Time]: Issue reported
- [Time]: Root cause identified
- [Time]: Fix deployed

**Updates**:
- ...
```

## Post-Incident Review Template

After any incident, document:
1. **Timeline**: When did it start, when detected, when resolved
2. **Impact**: How many users affected, which platforms
3. **Root Cause**: What went wrong
4. **Detection**: How was it discovered
5. **Resolution**: What fixed it
6. **Prevention**: How to prevent recurrence

## Files to Create

1. `/data/projects/homebrew-tap/docs/RECOVERY.md`
2. `/data/projects/scoop-bucket/docs/RECOVERY.md`
3. `/data/projects/homebrew-tap/.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/known-issue.md`
4. `/data/projects/homebrew-tap/.github/workflows/health-check.yml`
5. `/data/projects/scoop-bucket/.github/workflows/health-check.yml`

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Recovery procedures documented and tested
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks installed
- [ ] Health check workflows running
- [ ] Issue templates created
- [ ] Post-incident review template available
- [ ] All procedures tested with simulated failures

 {"id":"bd-aw89","title":"Audit smartedgar_mcp for package distribution","description":"# Audit: smartedgar_mcp for Package Distribution

## Tool Overview

**Repository**: /data/projects/smartedgar_mcp (https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/smartedgar_mcp)
**Language**: Python
**Purpose**: SEC EDGAR MCP server with rich document processing

SmartEdgar is a modern SEC EDGAR ingestion, processing, and research platform:
- High-throughput filing downloader (10-K, 10-Q, 8-K, S-1, XBRL)
- Rich document processing (PyMuPDF, XBRL toolchains, PDF/HTML parsers)
- MCP server integration for AI agent queries
- Typer-based CLI for human analysts

## Audit Checklist

### 1. Distribution Approach Analysis

Like mcp_agent_mail, this is a Python project. Options:
- [ ] **Homebrew formula**: pip install into virtualenv
- [ ] **pipx**: Standard Python CLI distribution
- [ ] **Standalone binary**: PyInstaller (may be complex due to dependencies)

### 2. Current State
- [ ] Check for GitHub releases
- [ ] Check for PyPI publication
- [ ] Check pyproject.toml/setup.py
- [ ] Identify CLI entry points

### 3. Heavy Dependencies
SmartEdgar likely has significant dependencies:
- [ ] PyMuPDF for PDF processing
- [ ] XBRL parsing libraries
- [ ] Potentially ML models for analysis
- [ ] Database (SQLite? PostgreSQL?)

Heavy dependencies make standalone binary distribution challenging.

### 4. Server vs CLI Hybrid
- [ ] Document CLI commands (download, search, screen)
- [ ] Document MCP server mode
- [ ] Note any background processing requirements

## Platform Considerations

SEC EDGAR is US-focused, but the tool should work on:
- macOS: Primary development platform
- Linux: Server deployments
- Windows: May have more limited interest but should work

## Data Storage

SmartEdgar processes and stores filing data locally:
- [ ] Where is data stored by default?
- [ ] How much disk space needed?
- [ ] Any database setup required?

## Packaging Complexity

This is a complex application with:
- MCP server component
- CLI component
- Data processing pipeline
- Significant dependencies

May be better suited for:
1. pipx installation with documentation
2. Docker image for full-featured deployment
3. Simple Homebrew formula for CLI basics

## Expected Outcome

Determine best distribution strategy given:
1. Complexity of dependencies
2. Server + CLI hybrid nature
3. NOT on PyPI. Best strategy: Docker (primary), PyPI (secondary).  {"id":"bd-b00c","title":"Implement run_single_agent_workflow()","description":"# Single Agent Workflow Execution

## Parent Epic: bd-mkoc (Agent Sweep Command Implementation)

## Purpose
Execute the full three-phase agent workflow for a single repository.

## Implementation

```bash
run_single_agent_workflow() {
    local repo_path="$1"
    local with_release="${2:-false}"
    local start_time=$(date +%s%3N)

    # Preflight check
    if ! repo_preflight_check "$repo_path"; then
        write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "preflight" "skipped" 0 "$PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON" "$repo_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # ADDED: Create backup reference before any changes
    git -C "$repo_path" update-ref -m "agent-sweep backup before run $RUN_ID" \
        "refs/agent-sweep/pre-run-$RUN_ID" HEAD 2>/dev/null || true

    # Generate unique session name
    local session_name="ru_sweep_$(sanitize_session_name "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")")_$$"

    # Spawn session
    local spawn_result
    spawn_result=$(ntm_spawn_session "$session_name" "$repo_path")
    if ! json_get_field "$spawn_result" "success" | grep -q "true"; then
        write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "spawn" "failed" 0 "session spawn failed" "$repo_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # Phase 1: Understanding
    local phase1_prompt
    phase1_prompt=$(get_phase1_prompt "$repo_path")
    ntm_send_prompt "$session_name" "$phase1_prompt"

    if ! ntm_wait_completion "$session_name" "${PHASE1_TIMEOUT:-180}"; then
        ntm_kill_session "$session_name"
        write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "phase1" "timeout" "$PHASE1_TIMEOUT" "" "$repo_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract and validate understanding
    local pane_output
    pane_output=$(ntm_get_pane_output "$session_name")
    local understanding
    understanding=$(extract_plan_json "$pane_output" "UNDERSTANDING")

    # Phase 2: Commit Planning
    local phase2_prompt
    phase2_prompt=$(get_phase2_prompt "$repo_path")
    ntm_send_prompt "$session_name" "$phase2_prompt"

    if ! ntm_wait_completion "$session_name" "${PHASE2_TIMEOUT:-300}"; then
        ntm_kill_session "$session_name"
        write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "phase2" "timeout" "$PHASE2_TIMEOUT" "" "$repo_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract and validate commit plan
    pane_output=$(ntm_get_pane_output "$session_name")
    local commit_plan
    commit_plan=$(extract_plan_json "$pane_output" "COMMIT_PLAN")

    if ! validate_commit_plan "$commit_plan" "$repo_path"; then
        ntm_kill_session "$session_name"
        write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "validation" "failed" 0 "commit plan validation failed" "$repo_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # Execute commit plan (if apply mode)
    if [[ "${EXECUTION_MODE:-apply}" == "apply" ]]; then
        if ! execute_commit_plan "$commit_plan" "$repo_path"; then
            ntm_kill_session "$session_name"
            write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "execution" "failed" 0 "commit execution failed" "$repo_path"
            return 1
        fi
    fi

    # Phase 3: Release (optional)
    if [[ "$with_release" == true ]] && has_release_workflow "$repo_path"; then
        local phase3_prompt
        phase3_prompt=$(get_phase3_prompt "$repo_path")
        ntm_send_prompt "$session_name" "$phase3_prompt"

        if ! ntm_wait_completion "$session_name" "${PHASE3_TIMEOUT:-600}"; then
            ntm_kill_session "$session_name"
            write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "phase3" "timeout" "$PHASE3_TIMEOUT" "" "$repo_path"
            return 1
        fi

        # Extract and execute release plan
        pane_output=$(ntm_get_pane_output "$session_name")
        local release_plan
        release_plan=$(extract_plan_json "$pane_output" "RELEASE_PLAN")

        if [[ "${EXECUTION_MODE:-apply}" == "apply" ]]; then
            execute_release_plan "$release_plan" "$repo_path"
        fi
    fi

    # Cleanup
    ntm_kill_session "$session_name"

    local duration=$(($(date +%s%3N) - start_time))
    write_result "$(get_repo_name "$repo_path")" "agent-sweep" "success" "$duration" "" "$repo_path"
    mark_repo_completed "$repo_path"
    return 0
}
```

## Recovery Note
If something goes wrong, the backup ref can be used:
```bash
git show refs/agent-sweep/pre-run-<run-id>  # View original HEAD
git reset --hard refs/agent-sweep/pre-run-<run-id>  # Restore (use with care)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Creates backup reference before any changes
- [ ] Preflight check gates workflow entry
- [ ] All three phases execute in order
- [ ] Timeouts enforced per phase
- [ ] Plans extracted and validated
- [ ] Execution only in apply mode
- [ ] Results written for all outcomes
- [ ] Session cleaned up on completion/failure
